message:
Sync CI instructions in package publishing guide with tenzir/library setup

Update the GitHub Actions workflow and detect-touched-packages.sh script to
match the latest best practices from the Community Library. Changes improve
step naming clarity, handle shallow clones gracefully, add error handling for
base SHA resolution, and make the package detection script more robust and
readable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -96,7 +96,7 @@ Create a workflow that tests packages on pull requests and pushes. The following
 workflow mirrors the approach used in the Community Library.
 
 ```yaml title=".github/workflows/test.yml"
-name: Test Packages
+name: Tests
 
 on:
   workflow_dispatch:
@@ -106,24 +106,27 @@ on:
 
 jobs:
   test:
+    name: Test packages
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
-      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - name: Check out repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
         with:
           fetch-depth: 0
 
       - name: Install uv
         uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v5
 
       - name: Detect touched packages
-        id: detect
+        id: touched
+        shell: bash
         run: |
-          packages=$(.github/scripts/detect-touched-packages.sh "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}")
+          packages=$(.github/scripts/detect-touched-packages.sh "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || '' }}")
           echo "packages=$packages" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
 
-      - name: Run tests
-        if: steps.detect.outputs.packages != ''
-        run: uvx tenzir-test ${{ steps.detect.outputs.packages }}
+      - name: Run package tests
+        if: steps.touched.outputs.packages != ''
+        run: uvx tenzir-test ${{ steps.touched.outputs.packages }}
 ```
 
 ### Detect changed packages
@@ -132,24 +135,41 @@ Create a script that identifies which packages changed in a pull request:
 
 ```bash title=".github/scripts/detect-touched-packages.sh"
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
-set -euo pipefail
+set -eo pipefail
 
-base_sha="${1:-}"
+base_ref="${1:-}"
 
-if [[ -n "$base_sha" ]]; then
-  changed_files=$(git diff --name-only "$base_sha"...HEAD)
-else
-  changed_files=$(git ls-files)
+# Without a base ref, test all packages.
+if [[ -z "$base_ref" ]]; then
+  printf '.\n'
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+# If the diff fails (e.g., shallow clone), test all packages.
+if ! diff_output=$(git diff --name-only "${base_ref}"...HEAD 2>/dev/null); then
+  printf '.\n'
+  exit 0
 fi
 
-echo "$changed_files" \
-  | xargs -I{} dirname {} \
-  | sort -u \
-  | while read -r dir; do
-      if [[ -f "$dir/package.yaml" ]]; then
-        echo "$dir"
-      fi
-    done
+# Extract top-level directories from changed files.
+candidate_lines=$(printf '%s\n' "$diff_output" \
+  | awk -F/ 'NF>1 && $1 !~ /^\./ {print $1}' \
+  | sort -u)
+
+# Filter to directories that contain a package.yaml.
+filtered=()
+while IFS= read -r candidate; do
+  [[ -z "$candidate" ]] && continue
+  if [[ -f "$candidate/package.yaml" ]]; then
+    filtered+=("$candidate")
+  fi
+done <<< "$candidate_lines"
+
+if (( ${#filtered[@]} == 0 )); then
+  printf '\n'
+else
+  printf '%s\n' "${filtered[*]}"
+fi
 ```
 
 Make the script executable:
